rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF CONNIE M. MARATAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/8/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Notice was not given to the Court-appointed attorney for the conservatee. The accounting begins with a different balance than the ending balance shown to be on hand at the end of the temporary successor conservator's first accounting filed on 6/13/19 (which does not appear to have been amended). The non-cash assets have different numbers of shares and carry values. Paragraph 6 of the petition is not elucidating: the conservator does not disclose the current amount of bond or how much additional bond is necessary or when she filed her ex parte petition to increase the bond The ex parte petition appears to have gone astray after the clerk entered it into the ex parte tracking sheet. Counsel should send a new copy and a proposed order to the examiner assigned to review this accounting petition, as the correct amount of bond must be in place before an accounting can be approved. The receipts subtotals do not appear to total $267,305.99. The receipts shown on "Extra" Schedule A should have been inventoried and appraised. Original statements supporting those "receipts" must be filed. There is no Chase savings account ending in xx8217. Whose account is that? There was no order dated 12/19/19 authorizing payment of $95 to Johnston, Kinney, et al. Other disbursements need further clarification, such as: returned item fees; $1,000 to Aspelin & Bridgman for filing the Substitution of Attorney; no payee(s) identified for check or supply orders; payment to the tax collector without any description; a very high ATT bill on 11/19/29; repairs in connection with real property not held in the conservatorship estate, etc. Disbursements must show the payee AND purpose. There was no schedule of liabilities in the prior accounting. The disbursements on "Extra Schedule C" need additional support. As the Court Investigator pointed out, the conservator was not appointed general conservator until 7/17/19 and the temporary successor conservator's first account ended on 3/31/19, so this accounting should have reflected that it is the second (and final) accounting by the temporary successor conservator and the first by the general conservator. The courtesy copy provided to the court also does not appear to be a true and correct copy: page 1 of the accounting is followed by pages 35-48. The register of actions reflects nine Letters of Temporary Conservatorship, with the last one expiring on 7/11/19, not just one issued on 5/2/18 that expired on 4/25/19. The conservatorship or trust case number must be noted in parentheses under any pleading caption in the related matter. [LRSF 14.47 B.] Transfers of cash between accounts is NOT a change in the form of assets. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (an amended accounting appears to be required as well as supporting invoices for caregiver payments shown on Schedule C) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/28/22. [It would be helpful if disbursements to caregivers showed the date ranges for the services.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CONNIE MARY MARATAS TRUST 1997; AND THE CONNIE M. MARATAS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST 2014
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/8/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] The conservatorship or trust case number must be noted in parentheses under any pleading caption in the related matter. [LRSF 14.47 B.] Property on hand is missing the real property (fully described). Merely inserting the value on the summary of account is not sufficient. How does legal authority for payment of attorney fees in a conservatorship apply to a trust? Which order directed that disbursements for the Probate Referee (not Examiner) or to the surety be approved first? This relief is missing from the prayer ("Requests"). Changes in the form of assets (informational schedule F) should follow all other schedules. The shares reinvested do not show the value, which, when added to the value of the shares inventoried, should total the carry value at the end of the accounting period. The I&A reversed the credit union checking and savings account balances. Please file the Schwab account for the period preceding the date of appointment. The order bringing the trust under court supervision was signed on 12/10/20; it was filed on 12/15/20. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/28/22. (The attorney's declaration certifying retention of original documents refers to LRSF 14.100. There is no such rule number. Examiner's notes are not authority needing to be complied with; notes merely point out legal or procedural issues to be addressed.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT STAPLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Preliminary Distribution, Petition For Allowance Of Partial Statutory Compensation To Administrator And Her Attorney And For Reimbursement Of Attorneys Costs, First Account And Status Report Of Administrator And Petition For Their Approval, And Petition For Order Continuing Administration Of The Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/8/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Probate Code Sect. 11623(a)(2) limits the amount to no more than 50% of the net value of the estate. (There is no local rule allowing for a greater percentage.) Please file the invoices from HeirSearch and from the Australian attorneys. Receipts that were refunds or refunded are not includable in the fee base. Also, if a third party was paid to prepare the accounting, that amount must be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. 1) The proof of service filed on 5/11/22 (no courtesy copy provided) is incomplete at item 2 (address of the person who served the notice) and shows that notice of this third amended petition was mailed on 5/11/22, before the third amended petition was actually e-filed and available for viewing. 2) The decedent's sister resides in London. If she is a citizen of the UK, notice must be given to the consular official of the UK in the US. She is presumably still a Norwegian citizen just living in the UK, but that is not indicated anywhere. 3) The relationship to the decedent of the petitioner and of Eric Durr should be "stranger" as they are apparently not blood relations. They are also not "Executor" or "petitioning to be appointed Co-Executor", which are not relationships to a decedent or true.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 216 BEMIS STREET TRUST U/D/T DATED APRIL 4, 1953
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Terminate Trust; And For Instructions Regarding Plan For Distribution And Cred
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to take a first look at the pleadings and determine how best to proceed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 and LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE 1992 CHENG FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. The sole purpose of the hearing is to determine whether San Francisco is the proper venue. Please file a copy of the Probate Code 16061.7 notice prior to the hearing. Attorney Brett S. Lytle filed a Declaration Informing Court of Trust Proceeding in Other venue which alleged there is an (EX A) no such exhibit has been filed even after the issue was brought up by the examiner.

Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 06/19/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 07/03/2023.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CAROL ANNE HAMBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition For Order Authorizing Petitioner To Refinance Community Property Personal Residence
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Notice has not been filed. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.
